problem.title: House Robber
problem.description: You are a professional robber planning to rob houses along a street. Each house has a certain amount of money stashed, the only constraint stopping you from robbing each of them is that adjacent houses have security systems connected and it will automatically contact the police if two adjacent houses were broken into on the same night.
assumptions: Adjacent houses cannot both be robbed.
Return maximum amount you can rob.

example.explanation: Rob houses 1,3,5 (0-indexed: 0,2,4): 2+9+1=12. Cannot rob adjacent houses.

solutions.title: Solution 1 – Dynamic Programming [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: At each house the robber faces a binary choice: rob it (adding its value to the best result two houses back) or skip it (keeping the best result from the previous house). Building this recurrence in a dp array gives the optimal value at every position in linear time. The key insight is that the optimal substructure property holds: the best solution through house i depends only on dp[i-1] and dp[i-2].

solutions.title: Solution 2 – Space-Optimized DP [✓ Interviewer Accepted]
solutions.description: The recurrence rob(i) = max(nums[i] + rob(i-2), rob(i-1)) only needs the previous two values. Replace the O(n) array with two variables (prev2, prev1), updating them in-place. O(n) time, O(1) space.
